channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hundreds apply for restitution for abuse suffered at Florida reform schools
item.description: Hundreds of people who say they suffered physical or sexual abuse at two state-run reform schools in Florida are in line to receive tens of thousands of dollars in restitution from the state, after Florida lawmakers formally apologized for the horrors they endured as children more than 50 years ago.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Multiple people injured in balloon-drop incident at Massachusetts arcade
item.description: Cheers of excitement quickly changed to horror when the netted balloons pulled a large Lego display down onto the crowd below, including multiple children, Storyful reports.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KREX 5

item.title: Here are 5 hot-button laws taking effect in 2025
item.description: States across the country are bracing for new laws set to take effect as early as Wednesday, including some that have stirred controversy.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KRON4

item.title: Power outage leaves most of Puerto Rico in the dark on New Year's Eve
item.description: The majority of Puerto Rico was left in the dark on Tuesday after a power outage put a damper on New Year's Eve festivities earlier in the day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why you should probably throw away your Christmas tree this week
item.description: With the holiday season now behind us, it might be time to consider losing your Christmas trees, too.
item.dc:creator: Tylor Sorensen, Fox 5

item.title: Former Florida deputy identified as suspect in 1979 cold case, death of his wife: sheriff
item.description: A former deputy has been identified as the suspect in a 1979 cold case that left a 25-year-old store clerk dead, the Charlotte County Sheriff's Office announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Outage leaves most of Puerto Rico without power on New Year's Eve
item.description: Nearly 90 percent of 1.47 million clients across Puerto Rico were left in the dark, according to Luma Energy, a private company that oversees electricity transmission and distribution.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt reach divorce settlement after 8 years
item.description: Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt have reached a divorce settlement, ending one of the longest and most contentious divorces in Hollywood history but not every legal issue between the two.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Australia, New Zealand first to welcome 2025 with fireworks
item.description: The U.S. countdown is still hours away, but those in New Zealand have already started 2025 with a bang.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Have any New Year's resolutions? Here are some tips to help make them stick
item.description: Studies have shown that up to 70% of people who make New Year’s resolutions abandon those good intentions within months. Here are some tips for how to keep those resolutions when your willpower starts to falter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter sought to expand democracy worldwide long after he left the White House
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — Amid everything else on his desk — the Iran hostage crisis, domestic economic turmoil, the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan and a grueling 1980 reelection fight — President Jimmy Carter elevated the independence of a country in southern Africa as a top agenda item.
item.dc:creator: FARAI MUTSAKA and CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Migrant crisis added to record-level US homelessness: HUD
item.description: Sanctuary cities saw homelessness rates jump thanks in part to a surge of migrants being sent from the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Judge denies sentencing delay for ex-Sen. Bob Menendez but puts his wife's trial off until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez will be sentenced on corruption charges as scheduled at the end of January, and his wife’s trial will be moved from January to February, a judge said Monday. U.S. District Judge Sidney H.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Move over, Gen Alpha: 2025 marks the start of the Gen Beta era
item.description: If you just found out about Generation Alpha, brace yourself for a new crop of kids set to debut on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Sakshi Venkatraman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, WGN 9

item.title: The world population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after a 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year's Day, according to U.S. Census Bureau estimates released Monday. The 0.9% increase in 2024 was a slight slowdown from 2023, when the world population grew by 75 million people.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US on New Year's Eve
item.description: There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California ‘micronation’ offering reward after fathers, children allegedly burglarize, vandalize property
item.description: The “micronation” of Slowjamastan, located in Southern California, is offering a $1,000 cash reward to anyone who can help bring to justice a group of fathers and their children who allegedly burglarized and vandalized their property.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: LeBron James at 40: A milestone birthday arrives Monday for the NBA's all-time scoring leader
item.description: When LeBron James broke another NBA record earlier this month, the one for most regular-season minutes played in a career, his Los Angeles Lakers teammates handled the moment in typical locker room fashion. They made fun of him. “They told me I’m old as hell,” James quipped.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Linda Lavin, Tony-winning actor who starred in the sitcom ‘Alice,’ dies at 87
item.description: Linda Lavin, a Tony Award-winning stage actor who became a working class icon as a paper-hat wearing waitress on the TV sitcom “Alice,” has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter, the 39th US president, has died at 100
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, endured humbling defeat after one tumultuous term and then redefined life after the White House as a global humanitarian, has died. He was 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ALEX SANZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: State funeral for Jimmy Carter will be Jan. 9
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter has died at the age of 100. The 39th president of the United States was a Georgia peanut farmer who sought to restore trust in government when he assumed the presidency in 1977 and then built a reputation for tireless work as a humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Five defining moments of Jimmy Carter's presidency
item.description: Former President Carter's post-presidential life lasted decades, putting his career in the White House in a deep rearview mirror in many ways. Carter died Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Neukam, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter: A brief bio
item.description: — BIRTH NAME: James Earl Carter, Jr. — BORN: Oct. 1, 1924, at the Wise Clinic in Plains, Georgia, the first U.S. president born in a hospital. He would become the first president to live for an entire century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lakers send D'Angelo Russell to Nets in trade for Dorian Finney-Smith, Shake Milton
item.description: The Los Angeles Lakers have traded guard D'Angelo Russell to the Brooklyn Nets for forward Dorian Finney-Smith and guard Shake Milton. The Lakers also sent forward Maxwell Lewis and three second-round draft picks to Brooklyn on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Notable quotes by Jimmy Carter
item.description: Some quotations from Jimmy Carter. We have a tendency to exalt ourselves and to dwell on the weaknesses and mistakes of others. I have come to realize that in every person there is something fine and pure and noble, along with a desire for self-fulfillment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KRON4

item.title: Oregon men search for Sasquatch, die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KTLA 5

item.title: VIDEO: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Musk writes op-ed backing far-right German political party
item.description: The Tesla CEO said he has the “right” to address the country’s political climate as he has “made significant investments” in Germany’s technological and industrial sectors.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: These songs dominated the Billboard charts longest
item.description: Every now and then, a new song releases and dominates radio airwaves, Spotify playlists, and, of course, the esteemed Billboard Hot 100 chart—and in terms of those types of hits, 2024 has been pretty impressive.
item.dc:creator: Angela Underwood, Stacker, ABC 4

item.title: After predicting the financial growth of 2024, here's what 2 economists see in 2025
item.description: Brian Blank is a finance scholar and Fed watcher who researches how companies navigate downturns and make financial decisions, as well as how markets process information.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9
